title: See & Update All Tasks From One Place
excerpt:
I am on several boards where I can be assigned as the owner of a task. I would love to have one, workable place to view and update all of my tasks, regardless of on which board they were created. 
The nature of my role a…

posts.author: laurak (Laura Komenda)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am on several boards where I can be assigned as the owner of a task. I would love to have one, workable place to view and update all of my tasks, regardless of on which board they were created.

The nature of my role and team means that my tasks must exist on several different boards, but it makes it very difficult to see what’s on my plate.

Workarounds tried:

  1. My Week - requires due dates to be assigned before tasks show here. Not all of my tasks have due dates assigned, but I need visibility of them anyway.
  2. Dashboard - I’ve attempted to use both the Table (with multiple boards linked) and the Cross Account Board widgets on a dashboard to accomplish this, but there’s not a way to easily filter and/or save filters for an entire Dashboard. This would mean manually filtering each time to get the view I desire.

posts.author: Arnaud (Arnaud Prigent)
posts.cookedHtml: That would definitely help us too!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
laurak:

My Week - requires due dates to be assigned before tasks show here. Not all of my tasks have due dates assigned, but I need visibility of them anyway.

@laurak Try checking the box in My Week Settings to “Show assignments without a date”

posts.author: laurak (Laura Komenda)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the tip! That helps with seeing tasks all on one place but not with all the information associated with each task, and not the ability to update from there.

posts.author: nickv (Nick)
posts.cookedHtml: The My Week view is great. However, I’d like to be able to see ALL tasks (with dates) assigned to me without clicking forward 1 week at a time. Is there a different place this exists or a different way to achieve this, perhaps with a Dashboard?

posts.author: chrisz (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
This. If I’m not mistaken, I can’t believe there isn’t a way to achieve this!

I use the timeline feature to know when I should be working on something. It’s useless since it won’t appear until the week of the due date.

posts.author: jaykjapra (Jay Japra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed, having a “personal board” where all tasks assigned to an individual can be viewable in the same manner as a normal board seems a logical feature to have.
“My week” and “search all” for a particular name don’t go far enough to be as workable as a board view would. Seemingly the biggest constraint would be combining the various columns, but I think the easiest solution to that would be only include 1-1 columns or at the very minimum Pulse, Person, Status, Date.
If anyone ha other suggestions on what to include in such a board, it may help the Monday team figure out how to easily implement given the variability across boards.

Update: a possible workaround is to create a dashboard with the “Table” widget, select the various tables and groups you want to include, and set a filter where you are the “Person” (or whatever name you use to distinguish the owner of the pulse). You’ll have your own “personal board” of sorts!

posts.author: cquad (Camilla)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you, thank you, thank you so much for the idea of using the table widget!!! I finally have a good view of my tasks across all boards!!!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
jaykjapra:

Update: a possible workaround is to create a dashboard with the “Table” widget, select the various tables and groups you want to include, and set a filter where you are the “Person” (or whatever name you use to distinguish the owner of the pulse). You’ll have your own “personal board” of sorts!

I love this idea but it’s limited to a maximum of 10 boards on our account. We have dozens of boards.
I need it account-wide - all tasks on all boards with my name in the People column.

Still not possible. Why do such arbitrary limitations exist in Monday?

We have a Boltswitch and Search Everything so dozens of boards is obviously part of the platform design but a global overview of all my tasks account-wide is not.

For all its strengths, Monday is such frustrating software at times.

posts.author: Colton19 (Colton Dorris)
posts.cookedHtml: I honestly can’t believe this isn’t a feature. I just asked this in another post. Is there no way to even make this work through automation or item mirroring? I would be thrilled if me and my team could go through each project board on MONDAYS to pick out the weeks tasks and mirror/clone/still update both from one consolidated “Hot Items” board. Is this not a thing?

posts.author: chrisz (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second everything in this post!

Monday, please advise!

posts.author: BenH7778 (Ben)
posts.cookedHtml:
I could not agree more. This is so essential. A condensed view of all my tasks is essentially my to do list, and the main page I would reference.

I hate having to cycle through weeks in “My Week” view. I need to see everything assigned to me regardless of date, all in one place!

Please add this feature

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 from me. This functionality is crucial for any organization with staff who will be designated as owners of tasks across many different boards. This macro-level function should be available to each user to see their own tasks, and also to anyone else designated with permission (e.g., managers). For example, a manager of a program with 5 staff should be able to create a board (in table view) that show all tasks assigned to each of their 5 staff (including other basic fields like status and priority), and with tasks grouped by staff person. And these should all be editable directly from this macro board. Please add ASAP.

posts.author: angez (Angez)
posts.cookedHtml:
i logged in to the community just to upvote this!

please help build this function in <3 my team was previously on [another project management tool] and for the longest time after the switch to Monday.com, i couldn’t exactly put my finger to what i felt was lacking on Monday…

i realized through this post that it is the ability to have a detailed overview on all outstanding tasks. i personally feel that this is crucial for the task owners when they have multiple tasks spanning across different boards and scopes.

i have been trying to create them through my personal dashboard but it is really manual and you gotta select each board and task group carefully. when it’s crunch time, it’s really hard to set time aside to organize it this way! and why the search function doesn’t work… you can only search what you know and the list of outstanding tasks help you make sure that you haven’t missed other tasks from other boards while trying to clear tasks from a certain one!

thank you to the Monday team tho! really appreciate you guysss

posts.author: Shanerh (Shane Rhodes)
posts.cookedHtml: YES! Please!! I tried the “automation” mentioned on another feedback related to this but it didn’t work for me and couldn’t figure out the Tables view either. This seems like a basic item! What are all my items?!?!?!?! What do I need to get done- no matter what board they are on or when they are due.

posts.author: RobertS (Robert Sharrock)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 from me too. This is pretty make or break for us using Monday.

posts.author: chrisz (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday Team - can we get your input here?

This is a clear need and a basic feature that is offered through many project management platforms.

posts.author: gbradshaw (George)
posts.cookedHtml:
I signed up to upvote this update.

In my usecase, this is essential. I am subscribed to several boards, and knowing my upcoming tasks is crucial. I have set up a dashboard but you can’t sort by date, nor can you rearrange the boards depending on priority. This means a task that was added first due in December can appear at the top of a page, and a task added today due next week could appear all the way down the bottom - not very helpful for knowing what to prioritise on a day-to-day basis!

posts.author: gbradshaw (George)
posts.cookedHtml: You are unfortunately also unable to sort by date, or rearrange the boards - boards, groups, and the tasks within groups all appear in the order in which they were created.

posts.author: DanB (Dan Brazelton)
posts.cookedHtml:
We only use a couple of boards because of this limitation. All my tasks are in 1 board using groups - and each employee uses groups for different departments. So I can see all of my tasks - however, I can’t see all the tasks for all the projects.

Lacking this feature means we only use 50% of what Monday can offer - we can’t realistically have each project in it’s own board.

posts.author: Paul (Paul Barton)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be ideal for us too. We have hundreds of boards and a clear and simple way of seeing which pulses due. We use my week but if means us having to flip back weeks to find any missed tasks.

posts.author: leepatt (Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is SO important Monday - Can you please give us a response on timeline for implementation?
I have changed over my entire company to Monday from one of your competitors, the lack of this feature is a deal breaker and we are now considering changing back! Very embarrassing for both of us

posts.author: Adrian (Adrian F. Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree. I’ve mentioned this before. An item can be a few weeks long and I need to know when is that timeline for that item I expect to see items that need to be worked on, including when its due. I don’t want a task that should be worked on for three weeks to only pop up on the last week and then end up rushing my projects.

posts.author: noamca (Noam Carmel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday team,
This is a MUST HAVE feature for our teams as well.
We’re trying to move all our teams into Monday and not having this option can be a deal breaker for us.

Just a simple list view of all the tasks a user is assigned to, with a generated URL.

  • The ‘My week’ screen just isn’t usable if you want to see tasks with due date that isn’t in the current week.
  • The search everything also isn’t good enough as it has very limited filters, and there’s no URL.

Do you have any update on this feature?
Thanks.

posts.author: kos-la5 (Kris Sparks)
posts.cookedHtml: this is a useful feature to search over > 10 boards - though I would also like the Search Everything to have a way to include AND / OR conditions along with NOT - so that I could save a search such as: (items assigned to me OR my team) AND with Status NOT DONE could be displayed.

posts.author: OmerZ (Omer)
posts.cookedHtml:
noamca:

rything also isn’t good enough a

Would love to have this feature.

posts.author: Brigitte (Brigitte Gosens)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, I signed up just to upvote this topic.
This is one of the biggest frustrations of the entire team since we switched from another program to Monday. Hopefully will be solved in the near future.

posts.author: Marcoraiche (Marc-Olivier Raiche)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvoted. Idk what they are waiting for, so many users need this.

posts.author: Jorrit (Jorrit Heidbuchel)
posts.cookedHtml:
I love Monday and we have gradually moved almost all our data away from other systems and into Monday.

However, this function is also essential for my team. We have a different board for every proces in our company, but obviously not everyone is only assigned to only one proces and so everyone has tasks from various boards. For us, it is mostly crucial to see what to work on next. I don’t really mind if it would take going to the specific board to actually work on it, although it would be a plus if this view would allow working on the item, like the week view does with the pop-up window.
Many of our tasks don’t have specific due dates. We actually hardly ever work with due dates and work with priorities instead. It is somewhat frustrating that there seems to be no easy way to make a view that simply extracts every item in all of our Monday workspace with a specific person assigned as “owner” or “person”, and then sorts them according to another column value that in our case is either “low priority”, “medium priority” or “high priority”. It should in my opinion also definitely be possible to not organise all items according to their respective boards and groups, as the table view does. It doesn’t matter to me in what group or on what board my next task is, I just need to know what to work on next.

Thank you, Monday team, to create a great feature for this just like the many great features you already have!

posts.author: briennemartin (Brienne Martin)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvote here!! We have tons of construction management jobs with my job area spread across all of them! Need a central location for all my tasks!!

posts.author: jmks84 (Jonathan M)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been trying out project management software after project management software. This is a critical feature. If I have a task that is more than a week overdue, it does not appear on the default screen. Yes, I can work around it, some by using a table widget, but I need an easily accessed view where I can see all of my projects (and all of my team members’ projects) in one place to ensure no critical deadlines are missed.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
jmks84:

need an easily accessed view where I can see all of my projects

Agreed. My Week is great for a specific style of working. But I more often want to see My Tasks - all items assigned to me across all boards, all in one screen.

posts.author: Kristine (Kristine Sunde )
posts.cookedHtml: this is crucial. And I see that it’s been an ongoing topic for a long time, without any answer from Monday. What are all of you doing to solve this? Any other tool that is better? My boss is not so happy that he can’t get an overview on everything going on.

posts.author: thusitha (Thusitha Manathunga)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Kristine @jmks84 @briennemartin @Jorrit @Marcoraiche @Brigitte

Hi all,
You can use the 1View app to solve this issue.
Watch this demo to see how it works and with just 2 clicks you can add it to your monday account easily.
You can read more about this on here.

1View is still in its early stages and send your suggestions, feature requests and bug reports to support@serandibsoft.com.

posts.author: Marcoraiche (Marc-Olivier Raiche)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

It looks good, but we still have the problem that we can only connect 10 boards to it. Is there another way to work aroud?

posts.author: thusitha (Thusitha Manathunga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Marcoraiche,

Yes. That limitation is still there in monday.
We can allow for any board to be connected. But if it’s not connected with the dashboard, you won’t be able to update the item. That’s problem atm. For that reason only dashboard connected boards can be used inside 1View. We’ll see if there’s any workaround.

posts.author: Shanerh (Shane Rhodes)
posts.cookedHtml: I get a “this page is restricted” notice when trying to add the 1view app to monday.com. No worky.

posts.author: Shanerh (Shane Rhodes)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t believe this still isn’t possible in Monday.com! It’s such a simple seeming thing and yet they have hundreds of super complex pieces I will never use. Give us a simple way to put all our tasks in one place!!!

posts.author: anderslyman (Anders Lyman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

Anders from Tuesday’s Tools here.

I’m cross-posting here from a related thread: What am I assigned to that isn't already done (Personal to-do list/My Week) - #57 by thusitha

We’ve obtained permission from the Monday team to use all boards.
As such, we’ve added the following toggle to the top of our app:

This link should work to install: monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done

More info on our “Combined Board View” app: Filter across boards + bulk update with monday.com - #2 by anderslyman

Let us know if you decide to try it out!

posts.author: Ally (Ally Girone)
posts.cookedHtml: My team NEEDS this! I am used to working in something like Asana where you just have a straight to do list. I don’t want to look at every project every day. I want to know what I need to do next by date. Help!

posts.author: TuesdaysTools (Tomorrow's Tools)
posts.cookedHtml: @Ally sounds great! We can help you use the preview of our app. Please subscribe on our site and I’ll get in touch. tuesdaystools.com

posts.author: DTCSC (Danie Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please add this functionality.

I am regretting not going with Asana.

posts.author: DTCSC (Danie Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml: Also, it would be good to have assigned items tied to a persons email account rather than to the url account. For instance, I am apart of two accounts. Having the functionality of seeing my assigned items across all boards and accounts would be ideal.

posts.author: brittanyrolfe (Brittany Rolfe Hillard)
posts.cookedHtml: How is this NOT a feature in Monday?? How am I supposed to manage my tasks in this platform? I will never be able to fully adopt Monday to project management and collaboration until I can view & manage my tasks across all boards.

posts.author: NickVerri (Nick Kennedy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve just posted about this elsewhere, but yes. This really needs to be fixed. I’ve just come from using Wrike.com which is worse is loads of ways, but they get the My Tasks thing exactly right.

I want to see stuff that is overdue, due today, later this week and then in the future all in one place. And I’d love to be able to add tasks from this one page. Wrike gets around the date issue by automatically giving a new task todays date! Then if you aren’t worried about it, you can push it into the future, or if you need to get it done, it stays in your to do now bit.

I’d love to hear some progress on this. We’ve got a month by month subscribtion but this is preventing us from fully committing.

posts.author: Woody33 (Matthew Wood)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is an absolute priority for us! The entire team here can’t believe its not a native feature!

Can we please have some reassurance that this is on the roadmap and a potential live date?

posts.author: Woody33 (Matthew Wood)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree Nick. It would be great to get some reassurance from Monday on this feature and it it’s going to be included.

posts.author: engrswadood (Sultan Wadood)
posts.cookedHtml: I get the same error, did you find a solution?

posts.author: Ally (Ally Girone)
posts.cookedHtml: Apparently customers have been asking for this feature for years and it still hasn’t happened. I’m not holding my breath. Extremely disappointing.

posts.author: Shanerh (Shane Rhodes)
posts.cookedHtml: Nope, still get that message.

posts.author: DTCSC (Danie Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com Haven’t seen a response from Monday on this request.

posts.author: NickVerri (Nick Kennedy)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s super weird as My Week kind of does it, but in a broken janky way. It over complicates it and makes it less useful. I know I’m basically not using properly as I don’t have a decent task list at the moment, so its all a bit half hearted for our company at the moment.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
NickVerri:

I know I’m basically not using properly as I don’t have a decent task list at the moment, so its all a bit half hearted for our company at the moment.

Pretty much sums up our position too. My Week is too weak for our purposes. Broken and janky covers it.

Monday is so close to being a killer app but the lack of a robust, account-wide To-Do list that we can sort and filter in any way we like cripples it for us.

posts.author: echologist (Echologist)
posts.cookedHtml:
JohnW:

Monday is so close to being a killer app but the lack of a robust, account-wide To-Do list that we can sort and filter in any way we like cripples it for us.

That was what I was hoping as well. “My week” is pretty buggy. It shows Items which were already marked as done, there is no way to apply filter or see which tasks are beyond the deadline.

posts.author: DTCSC (Danie Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml: Happy 2021 @TRB-monday.com ! This request has gotten a lot of votes and people asking for this functionality. Is there any plan to include improved task management (that does not require the search function) in the roadmap? Thanks!

posts.author: peterledger (Peter)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com Yeah, we were just about to move from Asana to Monday, but the lack of a comprehensive “My Task List” is a deal killer. Monday is so much more robust than Asana, if they could only just have a simple task list…

posts.author: Guia (Guia Nocon)
posts.cookedHtml: Our team would also love this feature! It would be great to see new tasks pop up in my personal task board anytime a teammate assigns something to me from their own boards.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @DTCSC, happy 2021 to you too! And thanks for posting @peterledger and @Guia!

I’ve been doing some digging about this, and we are looking to open a feature that will allow you to manage all your tasks from a simple place. We definitely recognize the need for such a feature and are looking to release the first version of it this quarter. I don’t have more specifics at the moment on when exactly or what it will look like, but the feature should address many of the pain points you’ve described.

posts.author: DTCSC (Danie Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com That is great news! Looking forward to seeing what is developed When I used Todoist, they really excelled at task management across projects. Very simple and intuitive to use (daily, weekly, and monthly views). Also having something to capture todo items that can get recategorized later may be useful.

posts.author: Shanerh (Shane Rhodes)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow- that would be great! Been waiting for this so long and know our team would use Monday more with this possibility!

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, that is great news

posts.author: Ally (Ally Girone)
posts.cookedHtml:
YESSSSS, please make it happen! We’ve been waiting a LONG time.

I’m happy to be a beta tester!

posts.author: Fatbat (Brad)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve just joined this community to search for a solution to this problem. I recently joined an agency that has 40 workspaces with several boards each, and I could be assigned a task in any of them. I need a single place where I can see everything that has been assigned to me, otherwise this is completely unmanageable. It’s really driving me crazy.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s coming soon @Fatbat ! If you’d like to be among the first to hear about it, PM me and we can get connected.

posts.author: Cameron (Cameron)
posts.cookedHtml:
I sadly spent a fair amount of time this week setting up for a department wide trial to find there is no way for my employees to view all tasks assigned to them. This is a core requirement for us when selecting task management software.

@PolishedGeek - Is it possible to get some kind of beta access to this view?
I have been really impressed with Monday (apart from this) and would really like to find a way to make Monday work in our organization.

posts.author: sydney (Sydney Céspedes)
posts.cookedHtml: this is great news, would love an ongoing update on this, we are desperate for this!

posts.author: GlennKesteleyn (Glenn Kesteleyn)
posts.cookedHtml: Can’t believe this implementation seems to be so hard. Just bought PRO, almost regretting this if I see the need for this feature by the community, and the lack of implementation by Monday. Hoping to see some progress soon here.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @GlennKesteleyn - monday is currently revitalizing the previous My Week into My Work. You can turn it on under the monday labs area (click on your Profile and then on monday labs). When you turn My Work on, you are only turning it on for your user account, not all of your colleagues.

My Work is still in active development, so you’ll be seeing new options pop up over the next few months for additional filtering, custom groups, etc.

posts.author: LaunchMe (Shawna Culp)
posts.cookedHtml: The My Work beta doesn’t satisfy the use case of having one aggregate board for all tasks assigned to me, for which I can reprioritize/visualize. I need to see all my tasks on one board so that I can prioritize my tasks across multiple boards. I want to be able to filter/change view of my aggregated tasks so that I can use Kanban to visually prioritize my work. I want to see the changes I make on my Kanban reflected on the original board, but that the rearranging/views I do on My Work doesn’t affect the layout of the original board.

posts.author: scooper (Simeon Cooper)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TRB-monday.com This is the best news Monday.com could have, its the main downfall of Monday.com compared to its competitors like Asana etc that do this so easily! Next we just need a Project column that can mirror an entire task between Boards in one click rather than the mirror column function which is so clunky and time consuming!

https://community.monday.com/t/mirror-a-task-on-multiple-boards-with-a-project-column/6049

@laurak @chrisz @jaykjapra @angez @Shanerh @RobertS @Ally @DTCSC @peterledger

posts.author: CBice (CBice)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on this topic since December 2019???
I contacted the monday support on this need and they send me here,
I really need this, cant believe a basic overview of all workspaces / boards is not available especially when I see the price of my pro plan.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
@CBice Have you seen the beta feature My Work which Monday.com is currently testing?

Link to Help article for mobile version:

Support

Mobile app - My Work

3 min read Feature   My Work is a great way to see everything you and your team need to accomplish in a convenient, easy-to-use space. It works by showcasing all tasks across your monday.com ...

I have this available in Monday Labs.

Oddly, it defaults to sorting by Date Assigned which currently cannot be changed making it unusable for me. But I’m hoping it will improve soon.

posts.author: CBice (CBice)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks John, yes I did
but you cant filter by workspace or users, and there is so much spacing between each line I dont understand why they display it like that.
It is a half baked solution I hope they will improve soon, we are this close, on the verge of greatness

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
CBice:

It is a half baked solution I hope they will improve soon, we are this close, on the verge of greatness

Sums up my thoughts too. I really hope they make it usable in the real world before releasing it.

posts.author: scooper (Simeon Cooper)
posts.cookedHtml:
@CBice @JohnW Agreed this My Work Beta function has 3 major issues that I believe I have outlined and provided easy solutions below and in the screen shot. @TRB-monday.com Please get Monday.com to update this fail of a function before releasing!

  1. Everything is siloed broken into pointless assigned groups when due date is most important. Solution: Remove these assigned groups so there is one clean list of tasks and make this a filter if people want it.

  2. The Board header between every task silos everything from being in one clean list. Solution: move this Board header to be a column to the right like Asana does and this will make every task in one clean list.

  3. Due to issues 1 and 2 siloing all the tasks rather than them being in one clean list you cannot click the top of say the due date column and sort by which tasks are due first. Solution: Fixing issues 1 and 2 above will put all tasks into one clean list and then like a normal board you should be able to sort by clicking the top of the column.

See these issues and solutions in attached screenshot.

Monday.com My Work Beta Issues&Improvements1908×940 106 KB

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @CBice thank you for the feedback particularly around the UI and thank you so much @scooper for the very helpful diagram! I have passed on your feedback to the developers responsible and know that they will take your feedback into consideration. Please let me know if you have any other ideas or if anything else comes up! We really appreciate you taking the time to give all this feedback and hope we can use it to make My Work the best it can be

posts.author: jonathandbem (Jonathan De Bem)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @TRB-monday.com ,

In my opinion, in order to make MyWork be better is simply to have it look like the table widget but with the ability to view all boards assigned to me. Or better yet have a location with all tasks across all boards and give us filtering options so we can not just filter ourselves but maybe our team as well for those that are managing a team. Adding a calendar view with all tasks to the side would be good as we could then assign our outstanding tasks to a date that we want as a Due Date or planned date, all in one place. Scoro does this very well with their Planner feature.

Also it would be great to give us the ability to have different views (specifically commumihub) on the item updates section. When I click on “Add View” nothing happens.

Screenshot 2021-05-10 at 11.07.431524×1626 126 KB

Also, is there a time schedule for upcoming improvements?

Cheers

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jonathandbem - I see we are scheduled to meet later this week.
I will make a note to show you some alternative ways to see task information that are available right now, while the My Work feature evolves. Looking forward!

~ Deb Cinkus

posts.author: Hollyc.lambert (Holly Lambert)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m also looking for a better alternative to My Week, My Work, Search Everything, and building custom dashboards for each team member. I’ve resorted to the latter, but am still have issues. Using the filter feature on these dashboards is really time-intensive and it is not a “set-it-and-forget-it” tool.

Our design department receives items (tasks) from every other department on many different boards. They need to have a place to see ALL their assigned tasks in order of DATE (date, deadline, task timeline, etc). Each task on this list should provide a one-click path to the original item within it’s board for more info.

Not having this feature is creating anxiety for my team and is unsustainable long-term.

posts.author: Ally (Ally Girone)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are leaving for Asana. I don’t know why that platform can figure this out and not Monday, especially after clients have been requesting it for years.

Such a shame.

posts.author: DTCSC (Danie Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just another note. It seems assigned sub items are not represented in my work beta.

Still very frustrated with the implementation of task management. Monday.com does some things so well that this issue around the inability to introduce an intuitive solution for team member task management is jarring. I 100% understand things are in development and being tweaked, but I feel like some of the core assumptions around user requirements is off.

posts.author: cquad (Camilla)

  2. Remove all those board names!! Put them into a column.
  3. Allow us to sort and visualize by due date.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Hollyc.lambert @DTCSC @cquad - yes this is a bit of a blind spot on Monday. What we have do w in the past for clients is build a master/child board approach. This allows for all data from the numerous child (project) boards to be available within a single master board.

This can be achieved through using connected boards and mirroring columns. In order to hook back to the project board, you could add a link column that uses the URL of the project board.

There are some drawbacks to this approach, however you then have a single task repository to report off of.

This is useful for dashboard reporting (not hitting your board maximum based on license tier), overview boards, but also for the my work feature as you only need to use the single board for filtering.

It’s not the perfect solution but it seems to tick the most boxes .

If you’d like more information feel free to DM me here, or shoot me a message at info@greenllamas.com.
Also, check us out online to learn more about what we do.

Thanks
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

That said, tried so many different workarounds. We do not need a workaround, we need a functional task management system in place.

We will be re-releasing My Work soon.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)

Hey @DTCSC, @jesrau, @V.B.Music, @MikeH, and all, we have just re-opened My Work to monday.labs! ( What is monday.labs? )

posts.author: DTCSC (Danie Tucker)

posts.author: zach1 (Zach)
posts.cookedHtml:
Started using the “My Week v2” that was just reopened.

Firstly, this is a MASSIVE improvement and it looks like the feedback @scooper provided was mostly integrated.

This view is a LOT cleaner, so THANK YOU Monday for continuing work on this.

That being said, I’m noticing a few items that I think would be helpful to many users workflow.

  1. Please re-introduce a filter option. Some of our users are assigned as a person to a pulse/task, but they aren’t actually assigned to work on that task depending on what the status is. If we can filter those status’ out, this would be massively helpful to actually see what my team members are assigned to.

  2. Different Status Columns. Would be great if we can add additional status’ or other columns to this board as well, to alleviate having to click into the task to see additional details (like a second status column or numbers column).

I realize it’s done the way it currently is to keep it clean, but most Monday users are already used to horizontal scrolling, so I don’t think anyone would mind some extra columns.

  1. Again, thank you for working on this. Once this is fully integrated (and not a beta feature), this is what’s going to keep us on Monday for a very long time. I already have ideas on how to re-jig our Monday framework (Right now, we basically have one BIG task board). Once this is fully available, we can finally separate into multiple boards and not fear that someone is going to miss a pulse/task assigned to them.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

We apologise for the delay in addressing this thread. We hear your comments, and are actively working on our community responsiveness so please bear with us - we appreciate your patience in advance.

Currently, we are in the process of scanning our feature requests, merging duplicate threads to consolidate all votes and closing out already supported features. This request, specifically
See & Update All Tasks From One Place was posted back in December 2019 and since then we have released My Work which we believe addresses this particular request. That said, we certainly understand that there is clearly a-lot we can improve on, and kindly ask that you open up a new thread specific to those my work improvements so we can address each of these individually, and close out aged feature requests that we now support.

We hope this make sense and appreciate your understanding in advance
